Justification: The kettle shall have a minimum capacity of 1.7 liters to accommodate typical household needs for boiling water for multiple servings of beverages or cooking.; A 1.7-liter capacity is a widely accepted standard for electric kettles, ensuring compatibility with user expectations and existing usage patterns.; This capacity balances the need for sufficient volume with reasonable dimensions and weight for ease of handling and storage.

The kettle shall boil 1.7 liters of water from ambient temperature (20°C) to boiling point (100°C) within 5 minutes.; A boiling time of 5 minutes or less is considered efficient for electric kettles and meets user expectations for quick beverage preparation.; Faster boiling times contribute to energy efficiency by minimizing the duration of high power consumption.

The kettle shall have a power rating between 1800W and 2200W to ensure efficient and rapid boiling.; This power range provides a good balance between boiling speed and avoiding excessive strain on standard household electrical circuits.; Higher wattage generally leads to faster boiling, but extremely high wattage can be impractical and less energy-efficient over time due to rapid heating cycles.

The exterior casing shall be constructed from sustainably sourced, food-grade, BPA-free recycled stainless steel or a durable, biodegradable bioplastic.; Using recycled stainless steel reduces reliance on virgin materials and minimizes the environmental impact of manufacturing.; Biodegradable bioplastics offer an alternative end-of-life solution, reducing landfill waste, provided they meet durability and safety standards.; BPA-free ensures no harmful chemicals leach into the water, prioritizing user health and safety.

The interior of the kettle and the heating element shall be made of food-grade stainless steel (e.g., 304 grade).; Stainless steel is durable, corrosion-resistant, easy to clean, and does not impart any taste to the water, ensuring water purity.; It is a widely accepted and safe material for direct contact with boiling water and is recyclable at the end of its life.

The kettle shall consume less than 0.5W in standby mode.; Minimizing standby power consumption is crucial for an energy-efficient appliance, reducing phantom load and contributing to sustainability.; This aligns with modern energy efficiency standards and user expectations for eco-friendly products.

The kettle shall feature an automatic shut-off mechanism that activates when the water reaches boiling point or when the kettle is lifted from its base.; Auto shut-off prevents over-boiling, saving energy and reducing the risk of dry boiling, which can damage the kettle and be a fire hazard.; This feature enhances safety and operational efficiency.

The kettle shall incorporate boil-dry protection to automatically shut off the heating element if no water is detected.; Boil-dry protection is a critical safety feature that prevents damage to the heating element and the appliance, extending its lifespan and reducing waste from premature failure.; It also prevents potential hazards associated with a dry heating element.

The kettle shall be designed to withstand a minimum of 3,000 boiling cycles.; A lifespan of 3,000 cycles ensures reasonable longevity for a kitchen appliance, reducing the frequency of replacement and thus minimizing waste.; This target is achievable with robust design and quality components.

The kettle, when empty, shall be able to withstand a drop from a height of 0.5 meters onto a hard surface (e.g., tile floor) without catastrophic failure.; This ensures that minor accidental drops during everyday use do not render the product unusable, contributing to its overall lifespan and reducing premature disposal.; The base and handle should be particularly robust.

The handle shall be ergonomically designed for a comfortable and secure grip, even when the kettle is full and hot.; An ergonomic handle reduces strain on the user's hand and wrist, improving ease of use and safety during pouring.; The material of the handle should be heat-resistant and non-slip.

The spout shall be designed for drip-free pouring, minimizing water spillage.; A well-designed spout enhances the user experience by preventing messy countertops and reducing water waste.; The angle and shape of the spout are critical for controlled pouring.

The lid shall open easily with a single button press or a smooth manual action, allowing for convenient filling and cleaning.; Easy lid operation improves user convenience and accessibility, especially for individuals with limited hand strength.; The lid should also seal securely during boiling to prevent steam escape.

The kettle shall have a wide opening for easy access to the interior for cleaning and descaling.; A removable filter and a smooth interior surface without complex crevices will facilitate thorough cleaning.; Ease of cleaning encourages regular maintenance, prolonging the kettle's life and ensuring hygienic water.; Dishwasher-safe components (excluding the base) where feasible should be considered.

A clear, easy-to-read water level indicator shall be present, with markings for minimum and maximum fill levels.; This prevents users from overfilling or underfilling the kettle, which can lead to inefficient boiling, safety issues, and potential damage.; The indicator should be visible from multiple angles.

The power base shall incorporate a cord storage system to neatly wrap and conceal the power cord when not in use.; Integrated cord storage improves aesthetics, tidiness, and safety by preventing tripping hazards and keeping the countertop clutter-free.; This is a standard feature that enhances user experience.

The kettle shall operate at a noise level not exceeding 60 dB during boiling.; A lower noise level contributes to a more pleasant user experience, especially in open-plan living spaces or during early morning use.; This is achievable with good insulation and design of the heating element and casing.

The kettle shall have a modern, minimalist design with a neutral color palette (e.g., brushed metal, white, black) to complement various kitchen decors.; Aesthetically pleasing design enhances user satisfaction and product desirability.; Focus on clean lines and uncluttered form factor.

The packaging shall be made from 100% recycled and recyclable materials, minimizing plastic use.; Sustainable packaging aligns with the product's eco-friendly ethos and reduces environmental impact from production and disposal.; Minimalist packaging design should be prioritized to reduce material usage.

The kettle shall be designed for disassembly, with clear labeling of materials to facilitate recycling.; Designing for disassembly allows for easier separation of different material types, improving the efficiency and effectiveness of recycling processes.; This reduces the amount of waste sent to landfill and promotes a circular economy.

The kettle shall meet relevant safety and energy efficiency certifications (e.g., CE, Energy Star, RoHS).; Compliance with recognized certifications assures users of the product's safety, quality, and environmental performance.; RoHS compliance ensures the absence of hazardous substances.

An optional feature for variable temperature control (e.g., 70°C, 80°C, 90°C, 100°C) shall be considered for users who require specific temperatures for different beverages (e.g., green tea, coffee).; Variable temperature control enhances user experience and can optimize brewing for delicate beverages, leading to better taste and potentially reduced energy consumption by not boiling unnecessarily.; This feature should be implemented with energy efficiency in mind.

A clear, non-intrusive indicator light shall be present to show when the kettle is on and heating.; The indicator light provides visual confirmation of the kettle's operational status, enhancing user awareness and safety.; It should be subtle enough not to be distracting.

The power base shall be stable and non-slip to prevent accidental tipping of the kettle.; A stable base is crucial for user safety, especially when handling a full kettle of hot water.; The connection between the kettle and base should be secure.
